description: Evaluates whether the model responded in an interactive, step-by-step way: asked questions, confirmed design, then provided implementation sketch with edge case. Designed so that a single-shot or non-interactive answer fails at least 30% of criteria (human_rating false).

criteria.criterion: Model asked at least one specific clarifying question before proposing the API (e.g. key format, value type, persistence, or error handling).
criteria.success_condition: Clarifying question(s) are present and clearly tied to the design (not generic).
criteria.failure_condition: No clarifying question, or only rhetorical/generic questions.
criteria.notes: Set human_rating true only if the response explicitly asks the user for input on at least one design choice.

criteria.criterion: Model proposed a minimal API (signatures or endpoints) and explicitly asked for user confirmation before implementing.
criteria.success_condition: API shape is stated and model asks something like 'Does this work?' or 'Shall I proceed?'.
criteria.failure_condition: Model jumps to code or full implementation without proposing and confirming API first.
criteria.notes: Set human_rating true only if there is a clear proposal plus a confirmation request.

criteria.criterion: Model did not output full implementation code in the first response.
criteria.success_condition: First substantive response contains questions and/or API proposal, not complete code.
criteria.failure_condition: First response includes full implementation or long code blocks without prior clarification.
criteria.notes: Designed to fail for single-shot code dumps; expect human_rating false for many Gemini runs.

criteria.criterion: After user input, model provided an implementation sketch or pseudocode (or code) that matches the agreed API.
criteria.success_condition: Implementation sketch or code is present and consistent with get/set/delete/list_keys (or agreed variant).
criteria.failure_condition: No implementation sketch/code, or it does not match the previously proposed API.
criteria.notes: Evaluate on the turn that follows user confirmation.

criteria.criterion: Model mentioned at least one edge case or failure mode (e.g. missing key, invalid value, concurrency).
criteria.success_condition: At least one concrete edge case or failure mode is named and briefly explained.
criteria.failure_condition: No mention of edge cases or failure modes.
criteria.notes: Single-shot answers often omit this; expect human_rating false in many cases.

criteria.criterion: Responses were structured in clear steps (e.g. numbered or separated by purpose: questions, proposal, implementation).
criteria.success_condition: Reader can identify distinct phases: clarification, proposal, implementation.
criteria.failure_condition: One long block of text/code with no clear separation of steps.
criteria.notes: Encourages interactive pacing; non-interactive answers often fail this.

criteria.criterion: Model avoided assuming unspecified details (key format, value type, persistence) without asking or stating the assumption.
criteria.success_condition: Either asked about key/value/persistence or explicitly stated 'I will assume X' before design.
criteria.failure_condition: Chose key format, value type, or persistence without asking or stating assumption.
criteria.notes: Typical Gemini single-shot assumes e.g. string keys, dict storage; rate false if no ask/statement.

criteria.criterion: Model invited further interaction (e.g. 'What would you like to change?' or 'Should I add Y?') at least once.
criteria.success_condition: At least one sentence invites the user to refine or extend the design.
criteria.failure_condition: Response ends without inviting follow-up or next steps.
criteria.notes: Designed for headroom; many answers will not explicitly invite follow-up.

criteria.criterion: API proposal included at least get, set, delete, and list_keys (or equivalent) as specified in the prompt.
criteria.success_condition: All four operations are present in the proposed API (names may vary slightly).
criteria.failure_condition: One or more of get/set/delete/list_keys missing from the proposal.
criteria.notes: Correctness check; interactive flow may still omit one if not yet confirmed.

criteria.criterion: Tone was collaborative (e.g. 'we could', 'shall I', 'your preference') rather than purely instructive.
criteria.success_condition: At least one phrase suggests collaboration or checking with the user.
criteria.failure_condition: Purely imperative or lecture-style with no collaboration.
criteria.notes: Supports interactivity; set human_rating false if tone is one-way instruction only.

usage_notes: When you score the Gemini response (and optionally the full thread), set human_rating to true or false for each criterion. The rubric is designed so that when applied to a typical Gemini answer (especially if not fully interactive), at least 30% of criteria (e.g. c3, c5, c6, c7, c8, c10) should have human_rating false, providing headroom for improvement.
